issues: Recent failures cluster inside the same phenotype family; widen quote assembly before another replay

continuation-veto-fair-mid.expected_failure_signature: If the veto suppresses fair-mid release too broadly, calm slices stay too expensive or the branch slides back into arb leakage.
continuation-veto-fair-mid.expected_win_condition: Preserve the raw anchor quote assembly while reducing calm-path overpricing enough to convert the improving variant into a promoted one.
continuation-veto-fair-mid.hidden_coupling_removed: Fair-mid recentering should not automatically inherit the same persistence memory that arms toxic-side quoting.
continuation-veto-fair-mid.why_not_coefficient_retune: This adds a new veto contract between continuation state and fair-mid updates instead of changing the strength of an existing alpha.

flow-alpha-fair-mid.expected_failure_signature: If the alpha state lags or overreacts, arb leakage returns or the strategy recenters too slowly and overprices calm flow.
flow-alpha-fair-mid.expected_win_condition: Improve overall edge by holding protective posture through toxic drift while preserving calm-slice performance.
flow-alpha-fair-mid.hidden_coupling_removed: Short-horizon spot jumps should not decide both fair-mid recentering speed and toxic continuation state.
flow-alpha-fair-mid.why_not_coefficient_retune: This adds a new fair-mid state with its own decay and veto logic instead of only changing spread weights.

inventory-price-reading-router.expected_failure_signature: If inventory centering still behaves like a broad passive rebate, quote_selectivity falls and retail_edge does not recover enough to clear the promotion margin.
inventory-price-reading-router.expected_win_condition: Beat the breakout gate with arb_loss_to_retail_gain below 0.11 while keeping retail_edge at or above baseline.
inventory-price-reading-router.hidden_coupling_removed: Inventory centering should not share the same visible release path as the toxic-side protection channel.
inventory-price-reading-router.why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes the route and visibility of the inventory channel rather than only reweighting the same additive fee surface.

inventory-triad-replay-control.expected_failure_signature: If the anchor itself no longer reproduces, the family should be treated as stale and the lane should pivot.
inventory-triad-replay-control.expected_win_condition: Reproduce the prior raw anchor profile closely enough to justify further work on this family.
inventory-triad-replay-control.hidden_coupling_removed: This is a replay control, so it intentionally removes the new synthesis layer and preserves the anchor routing unchanged.
inventory-triad-replay-control.why_not_coefficient_retune: This is not a retune; it is a control replay of the prior raw anchor on the fresh lane.

passive-refill-auction.expected_failure_signature: If the refill window stays open through renewed toxic flow, the branch replays a weak-slice-only gain or broad cheap-mode arb leakage without promotion.
passive-refill-auction.expected_win_condition: Lift weak calm slices above the raw anchor while keeping anti-arb discipline and the fresh lane above the 480 breakout gate.
passive-refill-auction.hidden_coupling_removed: Passive refill should not be earned and spent through the same calm-memory path as broad recovery rebates.
passive-refill-auction.why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes where and when safe-side relief can appear relative to the raw tri-channel anchor, instead of scaling the incumbent rebate coefficients.

queue-aware-stress-shell.expected_failure_signature: If the queue-aware trigger still fires on short noise, the branch recreates an over-spiky shell without enough extra edge.
queue-aware-stress-shell.expected_win_condition: Retain the raw anchor anti-arb profile while trimming broad fee drag enough to make the line promotable.
queue-aware-stress-shell.hidden_coupling_removed: The same event pulse should not both widen the shared floor and fully arm the continuation shell.
queue-aware-stress-shell.why_not_coefficient_retune: This introduces a new continuation-budget trigger keyed to aligned toxic persistence instead of a stronger version of the old shell.

regime-switchboard-router.expected_failure_signature: If the router flips too eagerly, the branch replays the cheap-mode arb leak seen in earlier quote-map experiments.
regime-switchboard-router.expected_win_condition: Keep the raw anchor anti-arb improvement while routing calm flow into a cheaper neutral or refill lane often enough to clear the promotion margin.
regime-switchboard-router.hidden_coupling_removed: Safe-side refill should not be implicitly blended into the same continuous quote map that arms toxic protection.
regime-switchboard-router.why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes how the quote is assembled across regimes instead of retuning the weights inside the existing three-channel replay.

recent_failures.change_summary: Add a slow flow-alpha memory and use it only to modulate quiet recentering while leaving the tri-channel quote assembly intact.

notes: arb leakage worsened materially vs incumbent

recent_failures.description: Add a slow flow-alpha state to the raw tri-channel anchor to slow toxic recentering and accelerate benign counterflow recentering.

recommended_next_batch.reason: Explore both screen_0008 and screen_0006 as distinct screen-stage planning anchors before collapsing back to one line.
Arb leakage remains a distinct failure mode; keep one branch directly targeting arb_edge or arb_loss_to_retail_gain.
Keep one branch focused on weak low-retail, low-volatility, or tail slices instead of only overall mean_edge.
Keep one branch with explicit quote-selectivity and fee-discipline guardrails so structural pivots fail fast.
Reserve one orthogonal pivot so the batch does not collapse into semantically different versions of the same phenotype.
